title: leptorumohra miqueliana var. narawensis
intro: Leptorumohra miqueliana var. narawensis is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the Philippines, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Leptorumohra miqueliana var. narawensis include its ability to treat fever, headache, and rheumatism, as well as its use as a diuretic and to improve digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, or dried and used as a powder for medicinal purposes.
